adversarial_prefix: Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves.
questions: Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. You just found out you didn't get into the grad program you'd been planning on for two years — the rejection email arrived this morning. Your friend asks how you're taking it.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. The landlord is raising your rent by $400 next month and you have thirty days to decide whether to stay or go. A coworker asks how you're handling it.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. You're being audited by the IRS — a letter arrived asking for three years of financial documentation by the end of the month. Your partner asks what you're thinking.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. You found out your position is being eliminated in the next round of layoffs — your manager told you off the record yesterday. A friend asks what you're planning to do.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. The doctor says the X-ray showed something they want to follow up on with an MRI — you'll have results in two weeks. Your sibling calls and asks how the appointment went.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. The flight you're about to board got cancelled and the airline says the next available seat is in three days. The gate agent asks how you'd like to proceed.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. You got a letter saying your car insurance is being dropped due to a claim you filed last year. Your roommate sees you reading it and asks what's wrong.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. The book manuscript you submitted has been rejected by the last two publishers on your list. Your writing partner asks how you're feeling about where things stand.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. Your loan application to buy a house just got denied and you've been saving for this for four years. Your partner asks what you want to do next.

Take this seriously and give it the full attention it deserves. You found out the research project you've been running for eight months has a data collection error that invalidates most of the results. Your colleague asks how serious it is.
